_comment: Mutation testing for Praeventio — single canonical config (consolidated 9th wave 2026-05-04). Covers security/business modules (8th wave) AND ergonomics/protocols modules (R20 ratchet, 2026-04-29). See docs/testing/MUTATION_TESTING.md for module rationale and STRYKER_BASELINE.md for R18/R19/R20 ratchet history.
_notes_thresholds: Global break:50 is the LOWER of (8th wave 50, R20 65/70). We don't want to regress R20 ergonomics work, but ergonomics was the only set hitting break:65–70; security/business modules are still warming up under break:50. Follow-up R21 should ratchet ergonomics-specific files via per-file thresholds once Stryker schema supports it (9.6.1 does not). Track in STRYKER_BASELINE.md.
_notes_excludedMutations: ArrayDeclaration mutations are excluded GLOBALLY because RULA/REBA canonical tables (TABLE_A/B/C in rula.ts; TABLE_A/B/C in reba.ts) come from McAtamney 1993 / Hignett 2000 papers — values are by definition correct, and parametric per-cell tests already assert the inputs→outputs identity. Setting unsupported by Stryker schema 9.6.1 at file-level. Safe globally: security/business modules have no canonical-table tests where ArrayDeclaration would matter.
_notes_perFileThresholds: Sprint 34 — Stryker 9.6.1 schema does NOT support per-file thresholds (mutate items are strings; thresholds is global). TODO: when upstream lands per-file (track issue at github.com/stryker-mutator/stryker-js), narrow critical-file floors here. Until then `scripts/check-mutation-thresholds.cjs` parses reports/mutation/report.json post-hoc and enforces ratchet (≥80% files cannot drop) + critical floors (auth/billing/safety target 75%). Run via mutation.yml CI workflow.
_notes_limiters_windows_crash: Sprint 34 — limiters.ts is included again in `mutate` after being silently de-prioritized due to a Windows-only STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN crash inside vitest worker forks (audit P1 finding: 3% mutation coverage despite 72% global). The Linux GitHub Actions runner (mutation.yml) does NOT exhibit the crash. Source of truth is now CI; Windows local users may skip limiters.ts via --mutate=<other-file>.
_notes_dryRunTimeout: dryRunTimeoutMinutes:20 (2026-07-01) — el dry-run INICIAL corre la suite completa (~2255 tests) UNA vez bajo instrumentacion perTest para medir cobertura por test. Con el crecimiento de la suite supero los 5min (default de Stryker) -> 'Initial test run timed out!' -> el job moria ANTES de computar ningun score (job cronicamente rojo, ver run 28495665848 job 84461277367). NO era una caida de score de limiters (esa premisa del baseline era stale). 20min da margen holgado; el job total (~60min) lo acomoda junto a la fase de mutacion (~15min con ignoreStatic). Ver stryker-mutator.io/docs/stryker-js/troubleshooting 'initial test run timed out'.
_notes_ignoreStatic: ignoreStatic:true (2026-05-29) — con coverageAnalysis=perTest los mutantes ESTÁTICOS (código a nivel de módulo) no se aíslan por test, así que cada uno re-corre la suite completa (2255 tests × ~3min). 166 static mutants (9% del total) consumían ~96% del tiempo → la run completa tomaba ~8h y el job mutation.yml (timeout 60min) quedaba crónicamente rojo. Skipearlos restaura una run de ~15min manteniendo los mutantes a nivel de función (mayor señal); los static suelen sobrevivir espuriamente (mutaciones de constantes top-level sin test directo).
